Home / Internal Tools & Admin Panels / Alternatives to Retool

4 open source alternatives to Retool

Build internal admin panels and tools fast. Here are the open source projects real teams use instead — ranked by fit, with honest pros and cons for each.

What people don't love about Retool

  • Per-user pricing adds up.
  • Cloud-hosted by default; self-host only on higher tier.
  • Lock-in to Retool's component model.

Current Retool pricing (for reference): Free up to 5 users; Team from $10/user/month + usage.

Ad slot — above comparison

Quick comparison

Alternative Best for License Self-host Hosted cloud?
Appsmith
Open source Retool alternative for internal admin panels. 		The most Retool-like drag-and-drop builder, fully open source. Apache-2.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
ToolJet
Open source low-code builder for internal tools. 		Modern UI with git versioning for internal apps. AGPL-3.0 ★★★☆☆ Yes
Budibase
Open source internal tool platform with built-in database. 		Bundled database plus internal tool builder in one install. GPL-3.0 ★★☆☆☆ Yes
NocoDB
Open source Airtable alternative — turn any DB into a spreadsheet UI. 		When you mostly need a spreadsheet UI on top of your database. AGPL-3.0 ★☆☆☆☆ Yes

1. Appsmith — The most Retool-like drag-and-drop builder, fully open source.

Open source Retool alternative for internal admin panels.

Strengths

  • Drag-and-drop builder with JS scripting.
  • Wide data source connectors.
  • Self-host is free with no seat caps.

Weaknesses

  • Resource-intensive install (Mongo, Redis, etc.).
  • Complex apps can hit performance limits.
  • UI for versioning still maturing.
License: Apache-2.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

Appsmith homepage · Source on GitHub · Retool vs Appsmith →

2. ToolJet — Modern UI with git versioning for internal apps.

Open source low-code builder for internal tools.

Strengths

  • Modern UI and component library.
  • Multi-database support out of the box.
  • Git versioning for apps.

Weaknesses

  • Self-host has several services.
  • Some advanced features gated to cloud.
  • Learning curve for JS transformations.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 3/5 Hosted cloud option

ToolJet homepage · Source on GitHub · Retool vs ToolJet →

3. Budibase — Bundled database plus internal tool builder in one install.

Open source internal tool platform with built-in database.

Strengths

  • Bundled BudibaseDB — no external DB required.
  • Easy self-host via docker-compose.
  • Forms, dashboards, and workflows supported.

Weaknesses

  • Pricing for cloud premium features.
  • Smaller component library than Retool.
  • Some external DB connectors still maturing.
License: GPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 2/5 Hosted cloud option

Budibase homepage · Source on GitHub · Retool vs Budibase →

4. NocoDB — When you mostly need a spreadsheet UI on top of your database.

Open source Airtable alternative — turn any DB into a spreadsheet UI.

Strengths

  • Works on top of your existing SQL database.
  • Quick docker deploy.
  • Multi-view (grid, kanban, gallery).

Weaknesses

  • UI polish lags Airtable.
  • Advanced automation is in the paid tier.
  • Occasional migration friction between releases.
License: AGPL-3.0 Self-host difficulty: 1/5 Hosted cloud option

NocoDB homepage · Source on GitHub · Retool vs NocoDB →
